title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. paleacea
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. paleacea, commonly known as the pale lady fern, is a subspecies of the male fern (Dryopteris filix-mas), a type of perennial fern native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The pale lady fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. Its health benefits include the potential to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the pale lady fern is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with other medications and the risk of allergic reactions.
